@Procertus-ui-experiment/apps/frontend-pt1-extranet-onboarding/src I have scaffolded a new frontend application that serves as a prototype to implement a first iteration for internal and external testing of the new application, including the onboarding flow and certification request flow.  the flows are well documented in the analysis documentation. @procertus-docs/apps/frontend-docs/content/analysis/external-portal-cim-external-portal-1-analyse 

 I want to start implementing the flow and data organization to build this application based on the feedback and discussions we had about the first data delivery by Procertus. @procertus-docs/apps/frontend-docs/content/meetings/stavaza-04-2026-04-24.md @procertus-docs/apps/frontend-docs/src/analysis/data/procertus-categorization.json @procertus-docs/apps/frontend-docs/src/components/analysis/procertus-categorization-tree.tsx 

 as you can see in the repository where we will implement this prototype, we have already set up an extensive design system implementation using Shadcn and Tailwind. @Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui @Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui-lib  Together with the Tailwind team's implementation and the customization of the Shadcn primitives, we have drafted extensive guideline documents on how to use these components and apply the design guidelines throughout applications.

In this prototype, I want to adhere to these guidelines and set up everything using the underlying customized primitives from the UI package. When developing new components, I want to develop them in isolation within the UI library so they can be thoroughly tested using Storybook and documented for how to use these custom presentational components.

When we identify needs—such as setting up the user onboarding flow or the certification request flow and management for custom components—we should scaffold these inside the UI library and document their usage before implementing or wiring the presentational components into the prototype application.  to find all of the guidelines, we have documented these specific storybooks and stories about the guidelines inside the Documentation Portal website. @procertus-docs/apps/frontend-docs/src/design/design-system-nav-data.ts:347-461  of course. Together with the high-level guidelines, we have also related these guidelines to the underlying token usage and design system token setup. 

 it is clear I want the prototype we implement to rely on these design system guidelines by first using the Tailwind theme from the UI package.

When implementing custom components, always use the underlying primitives from the UI package. Implement the guidelines correctly by using the correct heading levels and other appropriate tokens.

 I want you to plan a development plan to build this prototype application as a VIT frontend app using React with the underlying UI and UIlib packages.

Document and implement presentational components in the UIlib and wire them into the frontend application where we do the actual data binding.

 make sure that in your plan you also orchestrate which tasks can be run by using sub-agents with their own specific and contained briefing and context to implement these one-off tasks.

Supply all needed information in the context briefing for each sub-agent.
</user_query>

input.overview: Wire the new Vite app to the monorepo design system (`@procertus-ui/ui` + `@procertus-ui/ui-lib`), introduce a small typed data layer for the Procertus decision tree, build presentational flows in `ui-lib` with Storybook first, then bind state and navigation in `frontend-pt1-extranet-onboarding`—aligned with analysis docs, Stavaza #4 decisions, and documentation-portal guideline references.
input.plan:
# Extranet onboarding + certification prototype (Vite + ui-lib)

## Context you are building against

- **Flows (source of truth):** [onboarding-flow.mdx](file:///Users/psoudan/workspace/05-Procertus/procertus-docs/apps/frontend-docs/content/analysis/external-portal-cim-external-portal-1-analyse/onboarding-flow.mdx), [certification-request-flow.mdx](file:///Users/psoudan/workspace/05-Procertus/procertus-docs/apps/frontend-docs/content/analysis/external-portal-cim-external-portal-1-analyse/certification-request-flow.mdx), [request-management.mdx](file:///Users/psoudan/workspace/05-Procertus/procertus-docs/apps/frontend-docs/content/analysis/external-portal-cim-external-portal-1-analyse/request-management.mdx).
- **Stavaza #4 (2026-04-24) product decisions:** [stavaza-04-2026-04-24.md](file:///Users/psoudan/workspace/05-Procertus/procertus-docs/apps/frontend-docs/content/meetings/stavaza-04-2026-04-24.md) — wizard **starts with intent** (product-oriented vs non-product routes: ATG, innovation attest); **Procertus decision tree** is authoritative for drilldown (not Quality2Build as operational taxonomy); default UX **scoped to what Procertus offers**; manufacturer-centric mapping where applicable; SSD vs innovation-attest semantics; CE level meanings; Procertus-attest vs ATG clarified.
- **Decision tree data:** [procertus-categorization.json](file:///Users/psoudan/workspace/05-Procertus/procertus-docs/apps/frontend-docs/src/analysis/data/procertus-categorization.json) — `meta.wizard.entryPoints`: `regulated-certificate`, `atg-attest`, `innovation-attest-ssd`; cluster/product hierarchy under `clusters`.
- **Reference UI for taxonomy visualization (docs app, different workspace import):** [procertus-categorization-tree.tsx](file:///Users/psoudan/workspace/05-Procertus/procertus-docs/apps/frontend-docs/src/components/analysis/procertus-categorization-tree.tsx) — useful for **types**, certification cell semantics, and UX patterns; **reimplement in Procertus-ui-experiment** using `@procertus-ui/ui` primitives (not `@workspace/ui`).
- **Design guidelines index (Documentation Portal):** guideline groups in [design-system-nav-data.ts](file:///Users/psoudan/workspace/05-Procertus/procertus-docs/apps/frontend-docs/src/design/design-system-nav-data.ts) (lines 347–461) — Typography, Color, Gradient, Spacing, Radius, Shadow, Elevation, Motion, Iconography, Logotype, Document architecture. In-repo, consume the **same tokens** via [`@procertus-ui/ui/globals.css`](file:///Users/psoudan/workspace/05-Procertus/Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui/package.json) and primitives from [`packages/ui`](file:///Users/psoudan/workspace/05-Procertus/Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui).
- **Monorepo conventions:** [`frontend-workspace` skill](file:///Users/psoudan/workspace/05-Procertus/Procertus-ui-experiment/.claude/skills/frontend-workspace/SKILL.md) — workspace imports `@procertus-ui/ui` / `@procertus-ui/ui-lib`; composed components and Storybook live in **`packages/ui-lib`**; `scaffold workspace-ui wire-vite-app` is the intended way to attach Tailwind v4 + aliases (today [`apps/frontend-pt1-extranet-onboarding`](file:///Users/psoudan/workspace/05-Procertus/Procertus-ui-experiment/apps/frontend-pt1-extranet-onboarding) has **no** workspace UI deps yet — see [`package.json`](file:///Users/psoudan/workspace/05-Procertus/Procertus-ui-experiment/apps/frontend-pt1-extranet-onboarding/package.json)).

## Target architecture

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  subgraph app [frontend-pt1-extranet-onboarding]
    routes[Routes / step state]
    containers[Containers and hooks]
    mocks[Mock APIs and fixtures]
  end
  subgraph uilib [packages/ui-lib]
    stories[Storybook stories]
    presentational[Presentational components]
  end
  subgraph ui [packages/ui]
    primitives[Shadcn primitives and theme]
  end
  subgraph data [Data module]
    json[procertus-categorization.json]
    types[Types and selectors]
  end
  presentational --> primitives
  containers --> presentational
  containers --> types
  types --> json
  app --> uilib
  app --> data
```

- **`packages/ui-lib`:** Dumb components + props-only stories; optional small pure helpers co-located; **no** app routing; import only `@procertus-ui/ui` (+ local `cn` if needed). Follow typography/heading guidance from the design system (single `h1` per view, logical `h2`/`h3` for sections).
- **`apps/frontend-pt1-extranet-onboarding`:** Vite SPA — layout, routing or linear step machine, **data binding**, local persistence if needed (e.g. `sessionStorage` for prototype), and composition of ui-lib exports.
- **Data:** Prefer a **single copy** of the JSON under the experiment repo (e.g. `apps/frontend-pt1-extranet-onboarding/src/data/` or `packages/domain` if you want reuse later) with a short README or comment pointing to the canonical export in procertus-docs; keep schema types in a **`certification-intake`** (or similar) module imported by both app and ui-lib stories via workspace path or re-export.

## Phased delivery (prototype iteration 1)

**Phase 0 — Foundation (blocking)**

1. Wire the Vite app to workspace UI: add `workspace:*` deps on `@procertus-ui/ui` and `@procertus-ui/ui-lib`; align `vite.config.ts`, `tsconfig`, Tailwind v4 (`@tailwindcss/vite`), and import `@procertus-ui/ui/globals.css` from app entry CSS; mirror alias/dedupe patterns from [`packages/ui-lib/.storybook/main.ts`](file:///Users/psoudan/workspace/05-Procertus/Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui-lib/.storybook/main.ts) where applicable.
2. Add root/turbo convenience (optional): script to `dev` only this app, and ensure `build`/`lint` tasks are declared in the app `package.json` for Turbo.
3. Copy decision tree JSON + export TypeScript types (`TreeNode`, `ProcertusDoc`, certification/procedures shapes) aligned with the JSON — can be derived from the types in procertus-docs’ tree component.

**Phase 1 — Shared shell and onboarding (vertical slice)**

4. **ui-lib:** Stepper / page shell (title, description, primary/secondary actions) using Card, Button, Typography primitives — Storybook variants for “onboarding step” vs “wizard step”.
5. **ui-lib:** Onboarding screens as presentational building blocks: customer context form sections, origin/KYC placeholder panels, “enriched profile review” layout, intake summary layout (props-driven).
6. **app:** Linear flow matching onboarding-flow mermaid: entry → start → context → origin/KYC → profile review → **handoff to request wizard** → summary → submit mock → activation mock → minimal “post-activation” stub (links to Phase 2).

**Phase 2 — Certification request wizard (core differentiator)**

7. **ui-lib:** Intent entry screen (three entry points from `meta.wizard.entryPoints`) — presentational; stories with each selection.
8. **ui-lib:** Wizard steps for cluster navigation, product listing, multi-select of applicable product types, certification badges — reuse semantics from docs tree (`hasCertifiableChip`, status text) but styled with Procertus tokens.
9. **ui-lib:** Draft request list + row actions (edit/remove placeholders), package review screen.
10. **app:** Session state: wizard answers, selected products, draft list; pure functions for “valid next options” can start as **stubs** (full graph logic in a follow-up) but structure interfaces for future implementation.

**Phase 3 — Request management slice (light)**

11. **ui-lib:** Authenticated shell (sidebar or simple top nav using existing primitives if available), organization context banner, request table/cards.
12. **app:** Mock “authenticated” toggle + reuse the same wizard component from Phase 2.

**Cross-cutting**

- **Accessibility:** focus order, `aria-current` on steps, keyboard for collapsible tree if you ship one.
- **Documentation:** each new ui-lib surface gets a `.stories.tsx` with MDX description where helpful; link to portal guidelines in story descriptions (meta only — no need to duplicate long MDX in the experiment repo unless you want to).

## Sub-agent orchestration

Use **parallel** sub-agents only when outputs touch **disjoint paths**; run **sequential** agents when the same files conflict (e.g. single `vite.config.ts`). Below: **self-contained briefing** you can paste into each Task prompt (`subagent_type`: `generalPurpose` for implementation, `explore` for read-only audits).

---

### Sub-agent A — `wire-vite-design-system` (sequential, first)

**Goal:** [`apps/frontend-pt1-extranet-onboarding`](file:///Users/psoudan/workspace/05-Procertus/Procertus-ui-experiment/apps/frontend-pt1-extranet-onboarding) runs `bun run dev` with Tailwind + `@procertus-ui/ui` theme and can import `@procertus-ui/ui` / `@procertus-ui/ui-lib`.

**Constraints:** Bun; follow [`skills/frontend-workspace/SKILL.md`](file:///Users/psoudan/workspace/05-Procertus/Procertus-ui-experiment/skills/frontend-workspace/SKILL.md); prefer `scaffold workspace-ui wire-vite-app` if it matches this repo’s CLI version, else manually mirror ui-lib Storybook Vite setup; import `@procertus-ui/ui/globals.css` in app CSS; dedupe `react` / `react-dom` / `@procertus-ui/ui` in Vite; extend shared tsconfig from `@workspace/typescript-config` like other packages.

**Done when:** App renders one screen using `Button` + token-backed typography from the theme; `bun run build` in app succeeds.

---

### Sub-agent B — `decision-tree-data-module` (parallel with A only if paths disjoint; safer after A)

**Goal:** Add [`procertus-categorization.json`](file:///Users/psoudan/workspace/05-Procertus/procertus-docs/apps/frontend-docs/src/analysis/data/procertus-categorization.json) into the experiment repo and export typed accessors: document shape, `clusters`, `meta.wizard.entryPoints`, helpers to flatten/search nodes for the wizard.

**Constraints:** No React in this module; pure TypeScript; keep types aligned with JSON; document source file + date in a one-line comment.

**Done when:** Unit-less `tsc` passes; app can `import data from '…/procertus-categorization.json'` with types.

---

### Sub-agent C — `ui-lib-onboarding-shell` (after A)

**Goal:** Under [`packages/ui-lib/src`](file:///Users/psoudan/workspace/05-Procertus/Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui-lib/src), add a small folder (e.g. `extranet-onboarding/`) with presentational **shell** components: step header (correct heading levels), body slot, footer actions; Storybook stories with knobs for title/description/step index.

**Constraints:** Import primitives only from `@procertus-ui/ui`; export from [`packages/ui-lib/src/index.ts`](file:///Users/psoudan/workspace/05-Procertus/Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui-lib/src/index.ts); no business logic except presentation.

**Done when:** `bun run --cwd packages/ui-lib storybook` shows new stories; components documented in story description.

---

### Sub-agent D — `ui-lib-certification-wizard` (after A + B)

**Goal:** Presentational wizard pieces: intent picker (3 routes), cluster/product explorer (collapsible list or table), multi-select product types, certification chips row, draft list + empty states; stories with **mock props** (small in-memory fixtures, not full JSON in every story).

**Constraints:** Same as C; reference UX semantics from [procertus-categorization-tree.tsx](file:///Users/psoudan/workspace/05-Procertus/procertus-docs/apps/frontend-docs/src/components/analysis/procertus-categorization-tree.tsx) but **rebuild** with `@procertus-ui/ui` imports; reflect Stavaza #4: non-product routes visible at start; product path shows ATG etc. only when applicable (props-driven “availability” from parent).

**Done when:** Stories cover main states; no dependency on React Router.

---

### Sub-agent E — `app-flow-wiring` (after C + D + B)

**Goal:** In the Vite app, implement route or state machine: onboarding sequence + embedded wizard + mock submit/activation; bind to real JSON via B’s module; compose C/D components.

**Constraints:** Keep containers thin; local state or minimal reducer; no real backend; use design tokens for layout spacing.

**Done when:** Single happy-path demo from anonymous entry through draft creation to summary; optional mock “login” flip for request-management stub.

---

### Sub-agent F — `quality-pass` (optional, readonly `explore` or post-merge)

**Goal:** Run `oxlint`/eslint and fix a11y issues in new components; compare spacing/typography against guideline intent (Documentation Portal meta titles listed in design-system-nav-data).

**Constraints:** Read-only exploration first; then one focused fix PR.

---

## Risks and mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| Vite + Tailwind v4 + workspace aliases fragile | Copy proven patterns from ui-lib Storybook; dedupe React; avoid duplicate `@` alias hijacking `packages/ui` |
| Duplicated JSON drifting from docs | Document canonical path; single copy + periodic sync |
| Wizard logic complexity | Phase 2 starts with **props-driven** ui-lib + stubbed resolver; iterate resolver with tests later |
| Heading/a11y drift | Enforce in code review: one `h1` per screen via shell component API |

## Suggested order for the parent agent

`A → B → (C ∥ D partial)` then `D completion → E → F` — where `C` and early `D` can overlap only if they use separate directories under `ui-lib/src`.

todos.content: Sub-agent A: Wire frontend-pt1-extranet-onboarding to @procertus-ui/ui + Tailwind v4 + globals.css; verify dev/build.
Sub-agent B: Add procertus-categorization.json + TypeScript types/selectors for wizard consumption.
Sub-agent C: ui-lib onboarding shell + Storybook (heading hierarchy, actions slot).
Sub-agent D: ui-lib certification wizard presentational set + Storybook (intent, drilldown, drafts).
Sub-agent E: App state machine/routes, compose ui-lib, bind JSON, mock submission/auth stub.
Sub-agent F: Lint/a11y pass and guideline alignment notes in stories.

## Parent session orchestration (how to run this plan)

- **This chat (parent)** owns: research, plan updates, acceptance criteria, ordering, and resolving cross-cutting decisions. It does **not** assume sub-agents will re-discover repo layout or doc URLs—each handoff includes paths, constraints, and “done when”.
- **Sub-agents** are **single-purpose workers**: paste the briefing block for **one** id (A–G or F); wait until its exit criteria are met (or it reports a blocker); only then start the next sub-agent session.
- **Handoff packet** (parent copies into every sub-agent prompt): (1) which `todo` id; (2) the sub-agent’s **Goal / Constraints / Done when** from this plan; (3) absolute paths to every file they may edit; (4) one-line “do not touch” list for adjacent todos to avoid merge pain.
- **Parallelism:** safe after **A**: e.g. **B** (data-only) and **G** (ui-lib shell) can run in parallel if they use disjoint paths; **G** may touch `management-interface/app-shell/*`—do not parallelize another task that edits the same folder.

### Sub-agent G — `ui-lib-generic-app-shell` (after A; before or in parallel with B if paths stay disjoint)

**Goal:** Deliver a **reusable application shell** for authenticated (or “chrome”) experiences: outer layout, **navigation drawer** (collapsible sidebar / mobile sheet behavior via existing primitives), header region, and main content slot. The **consumer (app or story) owns the exact navigation structure**—links, groups, active state, icons—as **serializable props / a small typed navigation model**; the shell only renders that model using **underlying presentational pieces** built from `@procertus-ui/ui` (`SidebarProvider`, `Sidebar`, `SidebarMenu*`, `Collapsible`, etc.), not hard-coded product labels.

**Existing code to generalize (read first, then refactor or wrap):**

- [`packages/ui-lib/src/management-interface/app-shell/ManagementAppShell.tsx`](file:///Users/psoudan/workspace/05-Procertus/Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui-lib/src/management-interface/app-shell/ManagementAppShell.tsx) — composes `SidebarProvider`, sidebar, header, scroll shadow, `<main>`.
- [`packages/ui-lib/src/management-interface/app-shell/ManagementSidebar.tsx`](file:///Users/psoudan/workspace/05-Procertus/Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui-lib/src/management-interface/app-shell/ManagementSidebar.tsx) — rich example of drawer/nav; today mixes layout with **management-specific** sections (workspaces, nav groups, user menu). Extract **generic** sidebar/nav renderers that accept a **declarative nav config** (e.g. nested items with optional `children`, `href`/`onSelect`, `isActive`, optional `icon`), and keep `ManagementSidebar` as a thin composition **or** migrate stories to the generic shell with mock nav data.
- [`packages/ui-lib/src/management-interface/app-shell/ManagementAppShell.stories.tsx`](file:///Users/psoudan/workspace/05-Procertus/Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui-lib/src/management-interface/app-shell/ManagementAppShell.stories.tsx) — reference for Storybook layout (`fullscreen`) and mock nav payload.
- Optional contrast: [`packages/ui-lib/src/public-registry/app-shell/PublicRegistryAppShell.tsx`](file:///Users/psoudan/workspace/05-Procertus/Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui-lib/src/public-registry/app-shell/PublicRegistryAppShell.tsx) — different pattern (header/footer/FAB); do not merge into one component unless clearly justified—reuse tokens only.

**Constraints:** No React Router inside ui-lib; navigation targets are `href` strings and/or callbacks supplied by the app. One logical `h1` per main view remains the **page** responsibility; shell chrome uses appropriate landmark roles (`header`, `nav`, `main`). Export new public API from [`packages/ui-lib/src/index.ts`](file:///Users/psoudan/workspace/05-Procertus/Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui-lib/src/index.ts) or `management-interface/app-shell/index.ts` as appropriate.

**Done when:** (1) Storybook documents a **generic** shell story with **two different nav trees** swapped via controls/props; (2) drawer open/close and mobile behavior still work; (3) `ManagementAppShell` story still passes (either unchanged UX via wrapper or updated to generic layer); (4) `oxlint` clean for touched files.

# PT1 extranet prototype — AI agent briefing pack

This folder contains **pre-written instructions** for implementing the **Extranet onboarding + certification prototype** (Vite + `@procertus-ui/ui` + `@procertus-ui/ui-lib`). Each file is a **single-task handoff**: an agent should take **one** briefing, implement only that scope, and report back with changed paths and verification commands.

## Repositories

| Role | Path (machine-local) |
|------|----------------------|
| **Implementation monorepo** | `Procertus-ui-experiment` — apps under `apps/`, shared UI under `packages/` |
| **Analysis & meetings (reference)** | `procertus-docs` — this documentation app; content under `apps/frontend-docs/content/…` and `apps/frontend-docs/src/…` |

Agents implementing code work **inside `Procertus-ui-experiment`**. Agents may **read** `procertus-docs` for flow specs and JSON source-of-truth paths cited in the briefs.

## Suggested execution order

1. [Task A — Wire Vite to design system](./task-a-wire-vite-design-system.md)
2. [Task G — Generic application shell + navigation drawer](./task-g-ui-lib-generic-app-shell.md)
3. [Task B — Decision tree data module](./task-b-decision-tree-data-module.md)
4. [Task C — Onboarding / wizard step chrome (ui-lib)](./task-c-ui-lib-onboarding-shell.md)
5. [Task D — Certification wizard presentational components (ui-lib)](./task-d-ui-lib-certification-wizard.md)
6. [Task E — App flow wiring (prototype app)](./task-e-app-flow-wiring.md)
7. [Task F — Quality pass](./task-f-quality-pass.md)

**Parallelism (optional):** After Task A completes, **Task B** and **Task G** may run in parallel **only if** they touch disjoint paths (data under `apps/…/src/data` or `packages/domain` vs `packages/ui-lib/…/app-shell`). If Task G refactors `management-interface/app-shell/*`, do not parallelize another task that edits the same directory.

## Parent / orchestrator checklist

For each handoff, paste into the worker agent:

1. The **full contents** of the relevant task `.md` file (or a link plus instruction to read it from the repo).
2. Explicit **“do not implement”** list: all other task IDs.
3. The **definition of done** from that file’s end section.

## Conventions (all tasks)

- **Package manager:** Bun (`bun install`, `bun run`, `bunx`).
- **Imports:** `@procertus-ui/ui`, `@procertus-ui/ui-lib` (workspace packages).
- **UI composition:** New reusable **presentational** pieces live in `packages/ui-lib`; **primitives** only in `packages/ui` (Shadcn registry).
- **Design system:** Use `@procertus-ui/ui/globals.css` and token-backed Tailwind classes; respect heading hierarchy (one logical `h1` per screen where applicable).
- **Plan source:** Product and UX intent come from the external-portal analysis docs and Stavaza #4; file paths are referenced inside each task brief.

## Task index

| ID | File | Package / app |
|----|------|----------------|
| A | [task-a-wire-vite-design-system.md](./task-a-wire-vite-design-system.md) | `apps/frontend-pt1-extranet-onboarding` |
| G | [task-g-ui-lib-generic-app-shell.md](./task-g-ui-lib-generic-app-shell.md) | `packages/ui-lib` |
| B | [task-b-decision-tree-data-module.md](./task-b-decision-tree-data-module.md) | app `src/data` or `packages/domain` |
| C | [task-c-ui-lib-onboarding-shell.md](./task-c-ui-lib-onboarding-shell.md) | `packages/ui-lib` |
| D | [task-d-ui-lib-certification-wizard.md](./task-d-ui-lib-certification-wizard.md) | `packages/ui-lib` |
| E | [task-e-app-flow-wiring.md](./task-e-app-flow-wiring.md) | `apps/frontend-pt1-extranet-onboarding` |
| F | [task-f-quality-pass.md](./task-f-quality-pass.md) | repo-wide for touched files |

# Task A — Wire Vite prototype app to design system (`wire-vite-ui`)

**Task ID:** `wire-vite-ui`  
**Agent role:** Implement only this task. Do not implement tasks B, C, D, E, F, or G.

## Objective

Wire `apps/frontend-pt1-extranet-onboarding` so it runs with **Tailwind v4**, **`@procertus-ui/ui` theme** (`globals.css`), and can import **`@procertus-ui/ui`** and **`@procertus-ui/ui-lib`**. The app must **`bun run build`** successfully and render at least one screen using a **primitive `Button`** plus token-backed typography.

## Monorepo paths

| Item | Path |
|------|------|
| Monorepo root | `Procertus-ui-experiment/` |
| Target app | `Procertus-ui-experiment/apps/frontend-pt1-extranet-onboarding/` |
| UI package | `Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui/` |
| UI lib | `Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui-lib/` |
| Shared TS config | `Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/typescript-config/` |

## Dependencies (`apps/frontend-pt1-extranet-onboarding/package.json`)

- `dependencies`: `@procertus-ui/ui`: `workspace:*`, `@procertus-ui/ui-lib`: `workspace:*`, `react`, `react-dom` (versions aligned with monorepo).
- `devDependencies`: `tailwindcss` ^4.2.x, `@tailwindcss/vite` ^4.2.x (match `packages/ui` / `packages/ui-lib`), `@workspace/typescript-config`: `workspace:*`, TypeScript ~5.9.x to align with workspace, Vite + `@vitejs/plugin-react`.

## Vite (`vite.config.ts`)

- Plugins: `@vitejs/plugin-react`, `tailwindcss()` from `@tailwindcss/vite`.
- `resolve.dedupe`: include at least `react`, `react-dom`, `@procertus-ui/ui` (same pattern as `packages/ui-lib/.storybook/main.ts`).
- If workspace resolution fails in dev: `server.fs.allow` including the monorepo root (e.g. `path.resolve(__dirname, "../..")`).

## CSS entry (`src/index.css` or equivalent)

1. `@import "@procertus-ui/ui/globals.css";`  
   Export is declared in `packages/ui/package.json` as `"./globals.css": "./src/styles/globals.css"`.
2. After the import, ensure Tailwind scans **this app’s** sources, e.g.  
   `@source "./**/*.{ts,tsx}";`  
   (paths relative to the CSS file; adjust if the file lives elsewhere.)

`globals.css` already pulls in Tailwind, theme, and `@source` for `packages/ui` and `packages/ui-lib`; the app layer must not rely on scanning only those packages.

## TypeScript (`tsconfig.app.json`)

- Extend `@workspace/typescript-config/react-library.json` (same approach as `packages/ui-lib/tsconfig.json`).
- Include `vite/client` types, `resolveJsonModule: true` (for later JSON imports in Task B).
- If `tsc -b` fails on unused symbols **inside** re-exported `@procertus-ui/ui` sources when checking the app, you may set `noUnusedLocals` / `noUnusedParameters` to `false` in the **app** tsconfig only, and rely on ESLint for the app—or document a narrower import strategy. The acceptance criterion is a **green `bun run build`** for the app.

## App entry (`src/main.tsx`)

- Import global CSS.
- Wrap the tree with **`ThemeProvider`** from `@procertus-ui/ui` (see `packages/ui/src/components/theme-provider.tsx`).
- Optionally wrap with **`ModeProvider`** (`packages/ui/src/components/mode-provider.tsx`) for light/dark/system.

## Minimal UI (`src/App.tsx`)

- Remove Vite demo chrome if still present.
- Render a minimal layout: e.g. `Button` from `@procertus-ui/ui`, short copy using design tokens (`text-foreground`, `text-muted-foreground`, `bg-background`, spacing utilities).
- Do not import `@workspace/ui` (that alias belongs to other repos).

## Verification (required)

From monorepo root:

```bash
bun install
cd apps/frontend-pt1-extranet-onboarding && bun run build
```

Both must exit **0**.

## Out of scope

- Copying `procertus-categorization.json`, domain types beyond what’s needed for `resolveJsonModule`, routing, onboarding flows, generic app shell (Task G), Storybook changes in ui-lib for new features.

## Definition of done

- [ ] Workspace deps and Tailwind v4 wired as above.
- [ ] `globals.css` imported; app sources included via `@source`.
- [ ] `ThemeProvider` (and optionally `ModeProvider`) applied at root.
- [ ] `App.tsx` demonstrates `Button` + token classes.
- [ ] `bun run build` in the app succeeds.

## Optional automation

If the scaffold CLI in this monorepo exposes `scaffold workspace-ui wire-vite-app <appDir>`, you may use it **when it matches** this layout; otherwise implement manually. Skill reference: `Procertus-ui-experiment/skills/frontend-workspace/SKILL.md`.

# Task G — Generic application shell + navigation drawer (`ui-lib-generic-app-shell`)

**Task ID:** `ui-lib-generic-app-shell`  
**Agent role:** Implement only this task. Do not implement tasks A, B, C, D, E, or F (unless a one-line fix is required for build coherency—prefer not).

## Objective

Deliver a **reusable application shell** in `packages/ui-lib` for experiences that need **persistent chrome**: outer layout, **navigation drawer** (collapsible sidebar / mobile behavior via existing primitives), optional header region, and a **main content slot**. The **consumer** (prototype app or Storybook) supplies a **declarative navigation model** (groups, nested items, `href` and/or `onSelect`, `isActive`, optional icons). The shell **renders** that model using **`@procertus-ui/ui`** primitives only—**no hard-coded product labels** inside the generic layer.

## Monorepo paths

| Item | Path |
|------|------|
| Monorepo root | `Procertus-ui-experiment/` |
| Primary work area | `Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui-lib/src/` |
| Reference implementation | `…/packages/ui-lib/src/management-interface/app-shell/` |
| Barrel export | `…/packages/ui-lib/src/index.ts` and/or `management-interface/app-shell/index.ts` |

## Reference files (read before coding)

1. **`ManagementAppShell.tsx`** — `SidebarProvider`, sidebar + header composition, scroll shadow, `<main>` wrapper.
2. **`ManagementSidebar.tsx`** — Full example of drawer/nav using `Sidebar`, `SidebarMenu*`, `Collapsible`, `DropdownMenu`, Hugeicons, etc. Today it mixes **layout** with **management-specific** data (workspaces, named nav groups, user menu).
3. **`ManagementAppShell.stories.tsx`** — `layout: "fullscreen"`, mock `sidebarProps` / `headerProps` shape.
4. **`PublicRegistryAppShell.tsx`** (optional) — Different pattern (header/footer/FAB). **Do not** merge into one mega-shell unless there is a clear, documented reason; reuse **tokens** and patterns only.

## Design constraints

- **No React Router** inside ui-lib. Navigation is `href: string` and/or `onClick` / `onSelect` callbacks passed in props.
- **Landmarks:** use `header`, `nav`, `main` appropriately. A single logical **`h1` per page** remains the **page author’s** responsibility inside `children`; shell chrome should not steal the document outline (use `div`/`span` with classes or `aria-label` on `nav` as appropriate).
- **Imports:** `@procertus-ui/ui` only for primitives (and existing ui-lib `cn` from `./lib/utils` if needed).

## Technical direction

1. **Extract** a small **typed navigation model**, e.g.:
   - `NavItem`: `id`, `label`, `href?`, `onSelect?`, `isActive?`, `icon?` (type compatible with Hugeicons `IconSvgElement` if you use icons),
   - `NavGroup`: `label`, `items: NavItem[]` or nested `children`,
   - optional **header/footer** slots or typed props for user menu **as optional render props** so the generic shell stays data-driven.
2. Implement **presentational** components, e.g. `GenericAppShell`, `GenericAppSidebar`, `GenericAppHeader` (names your choice) that **only** render the model.
3. **Refactor path (choose one, document in PR/commit):**

**Task ID:** `ui-lib-wizard`  
**Agent role:** Implement only this task. Assume **Task A** (Vite wired) and **Task B** (typed JSON + helpers) exist or will exist—import types/helpers from the agreed module path; use **small fixtures** in Storybook, not the full JSON in every story.

## Objective

Build **presentational** pieces for the **shared certification request flow** in `packages/ui-lib`: intent entry, cluster/product exploration, multi-select, certification badges, draft list + empty states, package review layout. **No React Router.** No backend.

## Product rules (must reflect in props / stories)

From **Stavaza #4 (2026-04-24)** and the analysis subdoc **Certification Request Flow**:

- Wizard **starts by clarifying intent**: **product-oriented** path vs **non-product** routes (**ATG**, **innovation attest** / SSD wording per stakeholder clarification)—user must reach these **without** being forced through product-type selection first.
- **Procertus decision tree** (spreadsheet-derived model) is **authoritative** for product drilldown; **Quality2Build** is reference-only—do not present Q2B as the operational taxonomy.
- **Multi-select:** when multiple product types apply, user can select several; each selection becomes a **draft** line item (represent as props).

## Reference implementation (read-only, different package name)

In **procertus-docs**, `apps/frontend-docs/src/components/analysis/procertus-categorization-tree.tsx`:

- Types for certification cells, chip vs empty semantics, labels (CE, BENOR, ATG, SSD/innovation).
- **Reimplement** in `Procertus-ui-experiment` using **`@procertus-ui/ui`** only—do not import `@workspace/ui`.

## Suggested component split (adjust as needed)

1. **`CertificationIntentPicker`** — three entry points aligned with `meta.wizard.entryPoints` from JSON: `regulated-certificate`, `atg-attest`, `innovation-attest-ssd` (labels copy-friendly for NL/EN stub).
2. **`ClusterExplorer` / `ProductTreePanel`** — collapsible list or table; props: `nodes`, `expandedIds`, `onToggle`, `onSelectProduct`.
3. **`ProductMultiSelect`** — checklist or selectable rows; props: `options`, `selectedIds`, `onChange`.
4. **`CertificationBadgeRow`** — props-driven chips + “not offered” text; pure presentation from normalized booleans/strings supplied by parent.
5. **`DraftRequestList`** — list with edit/remove as **callbacks**; empty state story.
6. **`RequestPackageReview`** — read-only summary table/cards before submit.

## Storybook coverage

- Intent: all three entry states + selected state.
- Drilldown: collapsed/expanded, no products / many products.
- Drafts: empty, one, many rows.
- Review: at least one snapshot.

Use **small in-memory fixtures** (3–5 nodes), not the full 1.4k-line JSON per story.
